description: This figure presents X-ray diffraction (XRD) patterns of as-cast Fe–28Mn–9Al-1.2C-xCr austenitic low-density steel strips with varying chromium contents (x = 0, 3, 6, 9 wt%, labeled as 0Cr, 3Cr, 6Cr, and 9Cr from bottom to top). The patterns display diffraction intensity versus 2θ from 30° to 100°, revealing the phase evolution across the alloy series. All samples exhibit austenite (γ) as the primary matrix phase, characterized by peaks indexed as γ(111), γ(200), γ(220), γ(311), and γ(222), alongside δ-ferrite peaks including δ(110) at approximately 44.1° and δ(200). As Cr content increases from 0Cr to 9Cr, the relative intensity of the ferrite δ(110) peak progressively strengthens while the austenite lattice parameter decreases from 3.70 Å to 3.55 Å, indicating increased ferrite volume fractions from 10.2% to 19.6%. This trend confirms that Cr addition promotes ferrite formation during the FA (ferrite-to-austenite) solidification mode, altering the dual-phase microstructure balance while maintaining the face-centered cubic austenite matrix.
